terms.definition: A seven-layer reference model that describes how data moves from an application on one host to an application on another.
Transmission Control Protocol — connection-oriented transport that provides reliable delivery, ordering, and flow control.
User Datagram Protocol — connectionless transport with no guaranteed delivery; used when speed matters more than reliability.
A Layer 3 device that forwards packets between networks using IP addressing and routing tables.
Virtual LAN — logically segments a switched network at Layer 2 without requiring separate physical switches.
